question.content: For XeOF5-, PH2-, POCl3, and ClOF2+, determine the molecular geometry code around the first atom using VSEPR and report all four results. Output exactly one token sequence in the format CODE(species)*CODE(species)*CODE(species)*CODE(species), plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: one final result only. If the result naturally simplifies to a single value, give that value; otherwise give a single algebraic expression using the symbols defined in the problem. Do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Draw the four Lewis-structure skeletons with a chemical drawing toolkit and classify each geometry by VSEPR around the first atom.
step_1.key_point: Map each central atom to AXmEn.

step_3.content: XeOF5- has 7 electron domains at Xe (AX6E), so its molecular geometry is pentagonal pyramidal.

step_5.content: PH2- is AX2E2 at P, giving a bent molecular geometry.

step_7.content: POCl3 is AX4 at P, so the molecular geometry is tetrahedral.

step_9.content: ClOF2+ is AX3E at Cl, giving trigonal pyramidal geometry.
